description: Original tasks before rewriting based on actual data

tasks.name: Add the cheapest Angus heifer lot in Oklahoma under $1,800 closing within 3 days to your watchlist
tasks.description: Find Angus heifer listings located in Oklahoma that close within the next 3 days, filter to a maximum price of $1,800 per head, sort by price, and add the single cheapest qualifying lot to your watchlist.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage.
Click on the main search bar in the header.
Type "Angus heifer" into the search field and press Enter to run the search.
On the search results page, open the Location filter and select "Oklahoma" as the state.
Open the Price per head filter, set the maximum price field to "1800", and apply the filter.
Open the Closing date filter and select the option "Ending in the next 3 days".
Click the Sort dropdown and choose "Price per head: Low to High".
In the sorted results list, click the first lot card (the cheapest qualifying Angus heifer lot) to open its details page.
On the lot details page, click the "Add to Watchlist" button.

tasks.name: Bid on the largest feeder steer lot between 600–800 lbs under $2.20/lb
tasks.description: Search for feeder steers, filter to weights between 600–800 lbs, a minimum of 10 head, and a maximum price of $2.20 per pound, then sort by head count and place a maximum bid of $2.05 per pound on the lot with the highest head count that meets these criteria.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Auctions" menu and select the "Feeder cattle" category.
On the feeder cattle listings page, click into the keyword search box and type "feeder steers", then apply the search.
Open the Weight filter and set the minimum weight to "600" lbs and the maximum weight to "800" lbs, then apply the filter.
Open the Head count filter and set the minimum head count to "10", then apply the filter.
Open the Price filter and set the maximum price per pound to "2.20", then apply the filter.
Click the Sort dropdown and select "Head count: High to Low".
In the sorted results, click the first lot that is labeled as "Feeder steers" to open its details page.
On the lot details page, click the "Place Bid" or "Bid Now" button.
In the bid amount field, enter "2.05" as your maximum bid per pound.
Click the "Confirm Bid" button to submit the $2.05 per pound maximum bid for this lot.

tasks.name: Create a new auction listing for 15 Hereford bred cows in Kansas with a defined starting bid and reserve
tasks.description: Sign in as a seller and create a new auction listing titled "15 Hereford bred cows - Kansas" with quantity 15, breed Hereford, category bred cows, located in Dodge City, Kansas, with a starting bid of $1,200 per head, a reserve price of $1,400 per head, and a closing date exactly 10 days from today at 5:00 PM.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Sign In" button in the header.
In the email field, type "seller@example.com" and in the password field type "Password123!", then click the "Sign In" button.
After signing in, click the "Sell Livestock" or "Create Listing" option in the header or main navigation.
On the new listing form, click into the Title field and type "15 Hereford bred cows - Kansas".
In the Quantity or Head count field, enter "15" and in the Breed dropdown select "Hereford".
In the Category dropdown, select "Bred cows", then in the Location section choose "Kansas" as the state and "Dodge City" as the city.
In the Starting bid per head (USD) field, type "1200" and in the Reserve price per head (USD) field, type "1400".
Click into the Closing date/time field and select a date exactly 10 days from today, then set the closing time to "5:00 PM".
In the Description textarea, type a brief description such as "Group of 15 Hereford bred cows, 3–5 years old, synchronized and bred to Angus bull.", then click the "Publish Listing" button.

tasks.name: Edit an existing draft listing to change quantity, starting bid, and closing date
tasks.description: Open your draft listings, pick the first draft, and update it so that it has a head count of 18, a starting bid of $1,300 per head, and a closing date 3 days later than it currently shows, then save the changes.
steps: From any page while signed in, click your user name or avatar in the header and select "My Listings" from the dropdown menu.
On the My Listings page, use the Status filter to select "Draft" so only draft listings are shown.
In the draft listings table or grid, click the first listing in the list to open its details page.
On the draft listing page, click the "Edit Listing" button.
In the Quantity or Head count field, change the value to "18".
In the Starting bid per head (USD) field, replace the current value with "1300".
Click into the Closing date/time field and adjust it to a date exactly 3 days later than the currently set closing date, keeping the same closing time.
In the Description textarea, add the sentence "Updated lot size and pricing as of this week." at the end of the existing text.
Click the "Save Changes" button to update the draft listing.

tasks.name: Create a saved search for bred heifers near Austin with in-site alerts under $2,000
tasks.description: Set up a saved search for bred heifers within 100 miles of ZIP 78701, ending within 7 days, with a maximum price of $2,000 per head, name it "TX bred heifers under 2k", and enable in-site alerts for that search.
steps: Navigate to the website homepage and click the "Search all auctions" or similar global search link.
In the keyword search field, type "bred heifers" and apply the search.
Open the Location filter, enter ZIP code "78701" and set the radius field to "100" miles, then apply the filter.
Open the Price per head filter, set the maximum price to "2000", and apply the filter.
Open the Closing date filter and select the option "Ending within 7 days".
Above or next to the results list, click the "Save this search" button or link.
In the Saved search name field of the popup or form, type "TX bred heifers under 2k".
Check the checkbox labeled "Enable in-site alerts" or toggle the equivalent switch to the on position.
Click the "Save Search" button to store this saved search with alerts enabled.

tasks.name: Compare two Texas Angus bull lots and message the seller of the younger bull
tasks.description: Search for Angus bulls in Texas, look at the first two results, compare their ages, then contact the seller of whichever bull is younger with a specific inquiry message.
steps: From the homepage, click on the "Auctions" menu and select the "Bulls" category.
On the bulls listings page, click into the keyword field, type "Angus", and apply the search.
Open the Location filter and select "Texas" as the state, then apply the filter.
Click the Sort dropdown and select "Distance: Nearest first".
In the results list, click the first lot to open its details page.
On the lot details page, read and mentally note the bull's age in years, then use your browser's Back button to return to the results list.
Back on the results list, click the second lot to open its details page.

Compare the age shown for this second bull to the age of the first bull you noted; if the second bull is younger, click the "Contact Seller" button on this second bull's page, otherwise use the Back button to reopen the first bull's page and click its "Contact Seller" button.
In the message textarea, type: "I'm interested in this Angus bull. Please confirm current weight and fertility testing status."
Click the "Send Message" button to send your inquiry to the selected seller.

tasks.name: Build a watchlist of 3 affordable cow-calf pair lots for a trip in 2–3 weeks
tasks.description: Find upcoming live auctions for cow-calf pairs occurring 14–21 days from today, sort by lowest price per pair, and add the first three lots under $2,500 per pair to your watchlist, then open your watchlist page.
steps: Navigate to the homepage and click on the "Upcoming live auctions" section or link.
On the upcoming auctions page, open the Date filter and set the range to show auctions happening from exactly 14 days from today up to 21 days from today.
Open the Category or Class filter and select "Cow-calf pairs".
Click the Sort dropdown and choose "Price per pair: Low to High".
Scroll down through the results until you can see at least five cow-calf pair lots in the list.
Starting from the top of the sorted list, identify the first three lots whose price per pair is less than "2500" USD.

For each of these three qualifying lots, click the lot card to open its details page and then click the "Add to Watchlist" button, then use the Back button to return to the results list before opening the next lot.
After adding all three lots, click the "Watchlist" icon or link in the header to open your full watchlist page.

tasks.name: Register as a buyer and configure livestock interests and visibility settings
tasks.description: Create a buyer account with specific profile information (name, ranch, email, location), select feeder cattle, bred heifers, and stocker calves as interests, set preferred contact method to in-site messages only, and enable the option to show buying interests on your profile.
steps: From the homepage, click the "Register" or "Sign Up" button in the header.
On the registration form, select "Buyer" as the account type.
In the Full Name field, type "Jordan Cattlebuyer" and in the Ranch/Business Name field type "Circle J Ranch".
In the Email field, enter "jordan@example.com"; in the Password field, type "SecurePass!234"; and in the Confirm Password field, re-enter "SecurePass!234".
In the Location section, select "Nebraska" as the state and type "Broken Bow" into the City field.
In the Primary livestock interests checkboxes, select "Feeder cattle", "Bred heifers", and "Stocker calves".
In the Preferred contact method options, choose the radio button labeled "In-site messages only".
Click the "Create Account" button to complete registration.
After the account is created and you are signed in, click your name in the header and select "Profile Settings" from the dropdown.
On the Profile Settings page, toggle on the switch labeled "Show buying interests on profile" and then click the "Save Settings" button.
